Vitejs

JavaScript

Java

Webpack

Web Development

The most-downloaded build tool in the language topped every survey for three years. In June it got acquired, because the person who made it could not figure out how to pay for it.

7 min read
Just now

--

For three years in a row the same tool has been the number one choice in every developer survey. It is used in every new JavaScript project and it is the foundation that the whole stack is built on. In June 2026 something big happened to this tool. It was bought by someone.
The reason for this is that the person who created the tool, Vite could not work out how to pay for it to keep going. This is what success can look like in the world of open source software and Vite is a good example of this. Vite is an example of the strange things that can happen when something is really successful, in the open source world.
The numbers are not close.
Start with the survey because the difference is really funny. In the State of JavaScript 2025 which came out at the beginning of this year Vite got a 98 percent satisfaction score. Webpack, the tool that Vite replaced only got 26 percent, which’s down from 36 percent the year before.
